title: Urceolina lehmannii
intro: Urceolina lehmannii, also known as Lehmann's Urceolina or False Caladi, is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae, characterized by its yellow flowers and erect, green leaves. The health benefits of Urceolina lehmannii include its potential to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, as well as its ability to modulate the immune system. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Urceolina lehmannii is often used in traditional medicine, and it is commonly prepared as a tincture or infusion, although its use should be cautious and under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
taxonomy: Urceolina lehmannii is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaryllidaceae. It is native to South America and is known for its unique, trumpet-shaped flowers. Within the Amaryllidaceae family, Urceolina lehmannii is classified as part of the subfamily Allioideae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, including its leaf arrangement and flower structure. The Allioideae subfamily also includes other plant species commonly known as onions and garlic.
